key_insight: High-velocity air fuel (HVAF) spraying can produce thick NiTi coatings that retain martensitic transformation and functional shape-memory behavior, unlike many prior deposition methods.
engineering_relevance: This offers a practical route to wear- and corrosion-resistant smart coatings for components that need both surface protection and thermal/mechanical actuation.
method: HVAF-sprayed NiTi onto mild steel using four processing parameter sets
Characterized composition, porosity, microstructure, phase transformation, and thermomechanical response
Compared as-sprayed behavior with annealed coatings to assess functional recovery
evidence_signal: The coatings were evaluated by microstructural and phase-analysis measurements plus 3-point bending, nanoindentation, and scratch tests, showing martensitic transformation and actuation after annealing.
watch_item: As-sprayed coatings had inhomogeneous microstructure, voids, oxides, defects, and internal stress, so post-spray annealing and adhesion/strength limits remain important implementation concerns.
paper_title: HVAF Spraying of NiTi Coatings: Microstructure, Phase Transformation and Shape Memory Behavior
